# terrible_check_point_mgmt_cp_mgmt_best_practice_facts (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `best_practice_id` (String) Best Practice ID.
- `blade` (String) Returns all the relevant Best Practices of the selected Software Blades. When empty will return all the Best Practices.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `defined_by` (String) Returns all the relevant Best Practices of the selected type.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `details_level` (String) The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `gateway_name` (String) Returns all the relevant Best Practices of the selected Security Gateway object.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `limit` (Number) The maximal number of returned results. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects. Valid values are between 1 and 500.
- `name` (String) Best Practice Name. This parameter is relevant only for getting a specific object.
- `offset` (Number) Number of the results to initially skip. This parameter is relevant only for getting few objects.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `show_only_local_domain` (Boolean) Indicates whether the query should return only objects from the current local domain. This parameter is only valid for local domain.
- `show_regulations` (Boolean) Show the applicable regulations of the Best Practice.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `status` (String) Returns all the relevant best practices with the selected statuses. When empty will return all best practices.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `ansible_facts` (String) The checkpoint object facts.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
